programmes.show_description: Thieves are kidnapping people and harvesting organs in Benidorm, and after Madge is bundled into a car, the Garveys go on a frantic search for her. Joyce tries to impress a hotel inspector. S5 E4
Arthur senses a nice little earner when minder Terry is involved in a siege at a laundrette in the first episode of the classic series. S1 E1: Gunfight at the O.K. Laundrette
When Gran is at death's door, Alf is sure she wants him to take her valuable watch - to remember her by, of course. : Gran's Watch
George needs some money and so sells Mildred's sun lamp to Larry; later, when Mildred is upstairs, she sees her lamp and thinks that Robin has stolen it. S6 E4: The Sunshine Boys
The couples join a coach trip to the mountains where David buys Linda a gypsy shawl she has admired - when Amy finds that she has lost her money, David sells his watch. S1 E4: Spanish Lace
In tribute to Ozzy Osbourne, the first-ever episode of the original reality TV series. Ozzy appears on The Tonight Show with Jay Leno as a performer. S1 E1: A House Divided
Hit '70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E4: A Man and a Woman
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E5: Bottoms Up
Island railways have a particular fascination, and none more so than those on the Isle of Wight; we look at the changing face of the island's railways over the last forty years.

A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
When Tracey has enough of Dorien entertaining gentlemen callers and Sharon not bringing in a living wage, she throws them both out and they end up in Sharon's old bedsit. S12 E4: Going, Going, Gone
David is caught out when Amy tells him that Tommy Wright is dead, so he and Linda adopt disguises to avoid each other's spouses as they plan to move to another hotel. S3 E2: Deja Vu
November 1907: the loss of Emily has affected the staff a great deal, but Mrs Bridges appears to be so upset that she can no longer contain her emotions. S1 E9: Why Is Her Door Locked?
Aveline has an unfortunate experience on the catwalk, Freddie and Lilo Lill have a shouting match, and Joey is blackmailed. S3 E6
Harold has acquired a collection of classical music records and plans a cultural evening; Albert hates it so much that he takes some drastic action. S2 E5: A Musical Evening
The staff are up early to greet a new arrival at Hatley Station - another arrival later in the day is less welcome... S1 E7: Past Love
Classic sitcom about a miserly landlord. Rigsby faces the prospect of a lonely Christmas until Alan and Philip arrive unexpectedly. S2 E8: For the Man Who Has Everything (Christmas Special 1975)
Larry gives girls fake names; the most recent one he uses is Robin Tripp, so Chrissy and Jo think Robin is a real bounder when a girl turns up saying she is pregnant. S4 E4: Never Give Your Real Name
Gary attempts to have simultaneous dinner dates with Yvonne and Phoebe with a little help from Ron. S2 E4: Who's Taking You Home Tonight?
Jack comes back to Gallowshield and is shocked to hear of Matt Headley's death; he feels that Sarah's brother is hiding something and sets out to discover what it is. S4 E2: A Gift From Heaven
When an unscrupulous builder wants a piece of land occupied by a friend of Arthur's, he hires some thugs to put the frighteners on and tries to blame travellers. S4 E5: The Car Lot Baggers
John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Penny faces a testing time as she thinks that she may be pregnant and has to face dinner with Vince's parents. S2 E6: Pregnant?
Robin cooks a pork meal for Jo and her date not realising that her dinner guest is Jewish. S1 E3: Some Enchanted Evening
The staff continue the tradition of hosting and taking part in a cricket match, but preparations don't go quite as smoothly as expected. S2 E2
When Tracey has enough of Dorien entertaining gentlemen callers and Sharon not bringing in a living wage, she throws them both out and they end up in Sharon's old bedsit. S12 E4: Going, Going, Gone
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Harold has acquired a collection of classical music records and plans a cultural evening; Albert hates it so much that he takes some drastic action. S2 E5: A Musical Evening
Could rumours of the Grey Lady who haunts the house be true? Rigsby certainly believes so, but he has not seen her yet... S2 E7: Things That Go Bump In The Night
When Bob becomes more and more depressed by Thelma's absence and Terry's housekeeping, Terry can finally take no more and leaves him as well. S2 E11: The Go-Between
Arthur is only too willing to help when local eccentric Albert comes into an inheritance - but his help isn't necessarily needed. S6 E6: Waiting For Goddard
The British Olympic Synchronised Swimming team stay at the Solana, and are booked to perform in the pool. Their coach is not impressed with the hotel or Joyce's efforts, however. S5 E5
When Monica starts having visions of nursery rhyme characters, she knows what's going on - she's going to have another baby. S5 E3
Chrissy and Norman have been dating for some weeks to the annoyance of Robin, who goes on a disastrous double date with Larry. S6 E6: Fire Down Below
Rigsby is dressed to kill as he takes Miss Jones out to dinner to celebrate her birthday. S1 E3: A Night Out
The British Olympic Synchronised Swimming team stay at the Solana, and are booked to perform in the pool. Their coach is not impressed with the hotel or Joyce's efforts, however. S5 E5
When Arthur offers Terry the chance to make the easiest money he'll ever earn, it is clear there are going to be complications. S1 E2: Bury My Half at Waltham Green
Alf is feeling the effects of the demon drink. Else is unsure whether it was the single glass of port or the sausages that made him ill. S5 E6: Party Night
Robin's brother Norman comes to stay for two days and takes a shine to Chrissy; when the three of them go out to dinner, Robin gets food poisoning. S6 E5: Mum Always Liked You Best
Amy's suspicions are further aroused by David and Linda's midnight swim, and Robert also suspects that a romance is in the air. S1 E5: Bedroom Farce

In tribute to Ozzy Osbourne. The family's numerous dogs have been excreting in the house and ruining valuable furniture; the worst dog is Lola, a bulldog which belongs to Jack. S1 E2: Bark at the Moon
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E6: X Marks The Spot
Hit '70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E7: Something in the Night
This programme offers much rare footage made on the railways of the southeast of England between the 1930s and the 1960s.

Auntie Vera has got problems with her hips and her defective lifts and tells Sharon and Tracey she is moving in - there's only one room available, though. S12 E5: Tonight I'll Be Staying Here With You
The crew of the television holiday programme 'Wish You Were Here' - along with its presenter Judith Chalmers - are at the hotel. S3 E3: Close Up
It's the autumn of 1908 as Sarah returns while James and Elizabeth are helping the poor at a refuge; soon brought back to the house, Sarah stirs up trouble for all. S1 E10: A Voice From The Past
Freddie is arrested for stealing a dustcart, Grandad is taken to hospital, Billy quarrels with Julie and Jack is nearly seduced by Carmen. S3 E7
Harold has arranged a poker game with a few friends; Albert is morally outraged by the idea of gambling, but he sees that Harold is being cheated by his friends and decides to help. S2 E6: Full House
May is keen to make a career move to a bigger station, but both Jack and Cecil are set against her going and both try to apply for a transfer to be near her. S1 E8: Job Opportunity
Rigsby returns from a Spanish holiday to find Alan has let out the two spare rooms; he's happy Miss Jones has returned but less enthusiastic about a single mother and her baby. S3 E1: That's My Boy
Robin accompanies Chrissy to her nephew's christening while Jo runs out of excuses not to hear George sing in the British Legion's choir concert. S4 E5: The Tender Trap
When Yvonne lands her dream job up north, Gary feels he must finally choose between the two women he loves. S2 E5: Wish Me Luck
Jack is finding his feet and form again as social commentator Margaret Carter-Brown meets him and he shows her how the Depression affects families in Gallowshield. S4 E3: A Medal for the Argentine
Both Terry and Arthur are captivated by an Australian girl who claims to be searching for her long-lost boyfriend, but what is her real motive? S4 E6: If Money Be the Food of Love, Play On
John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Penny gets a divorce and a proposal on the same day. S2 E7: Goodbye Again
With Jo gone for the weekend, Chrissy frets over being alone in the flat with Robin. S1 E4: And Then There Were Two
Mrs Slocombe is stunned when her long-lost husband appears to have made enquiries about buying the hotel... especially since he's been missing since he went to a supermarket in 1942. S2 E3

Auntie Vera has got problems with her hips and her defective lifts and tells Sharon and Tracey she is moving in - there's only one room available, though. S12 E5: Tonight I'll Be Staying Here With You

Harold has arranged a poker game with a few friends; Albert is morally outraged by the idea of gambling, but he sees that Harold is being cheated by his friends and decides to help. S2 E6: Full House
Classic sitcom about a miserly landlord. Rigsby faces the prospect of a lonely Christmas until Alan and Philip arrive unexpectedly. S2 E8: For the Man Who Has Everything (Christmas Special 1975)
When faced with a fight, Terry has a golden rule: 'Do unto others as they would do unto you - but do it first'. S2E12: Conduct Unbecoming
Terry leaves his fingerprints on a stolen lorry while doing a job for Arthur, then panics at the prospect of retribution. S7 E1: It's a Sorry Lorry Morrie
As Madge prepares for her marriage to Mohammed, Mick and Michael discover all is not what it seems with the wealthy Moroccan suitor. Donald and Jacqueline play detectives, searching for a mole. S5 E6
When people think Frank's won the lottery, he's not about to say otherwise, with endless free drinks coming his way. S5 E4

Mildred is upset that she has not received an invitation to Norman and Chrissy's wedding, but George has hidden it from her because he wants to go to a darts match. S6 E7: Another Bride, Another Groom
Rigsby is given some advice from Alan and Philip - as well as some tablets - to calm his nerves in his amorous pursuit of Miss Jones. S1 E4: Charisma
